description: Review the features of skin and fascia.

cards.text: What are the two main layers of skin?
Where is thick skin found on the body?

cards.answer: The area of skin supplied by a single spinal nerve

tip.tip: Shingles rash follows one of these

cards.answer: Lines indicating the predominant orientation of collagen fibres in the dermis

tip.tip: Surgical incisions parallel to these heal better

cards.text: What is the thinnest skin in the body?

tip.tip: Despite being thin skin type, it is particularly delicate

cards.answer: Fat, cutaneous nerves, superficial blood vessels, and lymphatics

cards.text: What is deep fascia and what is it made of?
cards.answer: A tough membranous layer of dense connective tissue beneath the superficial fascia

cards.text: Which protein gives skin its strength?

tip.tip: Also found in tendons and bones

cards.text: What landmark dermatome level corresponds to the nipple?
What landmark dermatome level corresponds to the umbilicus?

tip.tip: Important for assessing spinal block levels
